title: corydalis bulbosa f. major
intro: Corydalis bulbosa f. major, also known as the greater corydalis, is a flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family. It is a perennial plant native to China, used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its alkaloid content, specifically berberine and tetrahydropalmatine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis bulbosa f. major have been associated with various health benefits, including pain relief, improved digestion, and enhanced cardiovascular health. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
